eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra demand is discount-driven (Subsidized Liquidation), Meesho shows volume at low price — no clean convergence.

convergences.title: Price-driven demand across channels
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (65% off) and Meesho (₹299) show demand only at deep discounts or low price points.
convergences.proves: The style appeals primarily to price-sensitive buyers.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style would sell at higher margins.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (8000+ units) at ₹299 with moderate rating, indicating value-fashion demand. If sourced at low cost, there may be a margin opportunity in tier-2/3 markets.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Nykaa absence and Myntra's subsidized liquidation signal no premium demand. The style's appeal is entirely price-driven; any buy-in at higher price points risks poor sell-through. Internal POS has no prior data to validate.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume with moderate rating; clean signal at low price.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: No clean convergence across sources; demand is entirely price-driven with no premium signal. Internal POS has no data to confirm. Wait for clearer signals or test at very low price point only.

eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation on Myntra; Meesho demand is genuine but at low price. Overall integrity is low due to discount distortion.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows explosive volume (52,000+ units) with accelerating reseller growth, indicating strong grassroots demand. If you can match the ₹199 price point, there is clear volume potential in tier-2/3/4 markets.

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium and value segments with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Strong demand across all channels
convergences.detail: Nykaa (premium, low discount), Myntra (mass retail, strategic pricing), Meesho (value, high volume) all show robust demand with minimal discount distortion.
convergences.proves: Golden Kurti has genuine demand across price tiers, not artificially inflated by discounts.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the same style can succeed at both ₹1450 and ₹349; may need separate SKUs.

convergences.title: Low discount distortion across sources
convergences.detail: Nykaa 5% off, Myntra 10% off (Strategic Value Pricing), Meesho no discount mentioned. No suspect or liquidation discounts.
convergences.cannot_prove: Future discount behavior or competitive pressure.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand signals across premium (Nykaa), mass retail (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels. Low discount levels indicate genuine style preference. Meesho's accelerating reseller growth suggests expanding reach into tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate external signals. The price gap between Nykaa (₹1450) and Meesho (₹349) may require separate product tiers. Myntra's rank 2 could be influenced by platform algorithms, though no noise flags were detected.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high rating, sufficient reviews.

myntra.reason: Strong presence, Strategic Value Pricing, high review velocity, no noise flags.

meesho.reason: Strong presence, high volume, accelerating reseller growth, good rating.

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot confirm or deny external signals.

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources show strong, clean demand with low discount distortion, indicating genuine style preference. No conflicts detected, supporting a wider rollout across multiple stores and regions.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will the same golden kurti design appeal to both premium (₹1450) and value (₹349) customers, or are separate SKUs needed?

eval-silent-meesho.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand strong on Nykaa, but Meesho absent and Myntra only moderate — demand may be niche, not mass

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho absent value presence
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong demand at ₹2200 with zero discount, high rating
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at moderate discount, clean signal
conflicts.meesho_says: No demand at all in price-sensitive market
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't scale to value-fashion customers?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists at premium price points with no discount dependency
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at lower price points or can be scaled beyond a few products

conflicts.proves: Some mass-market interest exists at moderate discount

convergences.title: Clean demand signals at premium and mass retail
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows zero-discount demand with high rating; Myntra shows low-discount demand with good rating. Both indicate genuine style preference, not price-driven.
convergences.proves: The style has authentic appeal at higher price points
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this appeal extends to value-conscious buyers

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra both show clean, non-discount-driven demand for niche kurtis, suggesting genuine style preference. If this trend can be captured at a competitive price, it may have legs in premium and mid-market segments.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence indicates no demand in price-sensitive tiers, and Myntra's moderate presence with only one product limits confidence. The trend may be too niche to scale broadly, and internal POS has no data to validate.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, zero discount, high rating, sufficient reviews

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, only 1 product, but clean discount context

eval-silent-meesho.bet_rationale: Premium and mid-market signals are clean but limited in scale; Meesho absence suggests value segment may not respond. A small trial in premium-focused stores can validate real demand without overcommitting.
eval-silent-meesho.key_uncertainty: Will the style resonate with value-fashion buyers if priced lower, or is it inherently premium-only?
